response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned, versatile engineer with strong software-craftsmanship habits (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and rare directly-relevant experience building IDE internals in C/C++ for NI LabWindows/CVI. However, the role centers on deep JVM expertise, Kotlin, and the Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate enterprise ecosystem, none of which the resume demonstrates in depth. Java appears only peripherally (robotic arms, device orchestration), with no Kotlin, Spring, or large-scale JVM tooling work. The core required stack is largely unmet, but the genuine IDE-internals background and engineering maturity make a screening conversation defensible.
strengths: Direct IDE-internals experience writing C/C++ components for the LabWindows/CVI IDE
Strong software-craftsmanship discipline: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based development
Versatile across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded; comfortable in complex, high-performance systems
concerns: No Kotlin and no demonstrated Spring/Quarkus/Hibernate enterprise-framework experience
Java exposure is peripheral; no evidence of deep JVM internals understanding
Recent and primary expertise is in TypeScript/Node and DLang, not the required JVM ecosystem
